summary:
The optic stalk is not located within or immediately around the floor of the third ventricle; it lies more anteriorly along with other structures such as the pituitary gland’s infundibulum and nearby neural elements including parts of CNIII. Structures closely associated include components related to neuroendocrine pathways rather than those primarily situated elsewhere above mentioned regions directly beneath the roof plate forming part of this brain compartment.
